evals.prompt: Create a single-file HTML page (import three from a CDN importmap) that shows a 3D product viewer: load https://raw.githubusercontent.com/KhronosGroup/glTF-Sample-Assets/main/Models/DamagedHelmet/glTF-Binary/DamagedHelmet.glb, studio-style environment lighting, soft shadow under the model, orbit controls limited so you can't go under the floor, and it must handle window resizing correctly.
evals.expected_output: Working HTML file: GLTFLoader + environment (RoomEnvironment or HDR), ACES/AgX tone mapping, shadow-catcher plane or ShadowMaterial, OrbitControls with maxPolarAngle, resize handler with updateProjectionMatrix, capped pixelRatio, delta-based loop.

evals.prompt: In my React app I have a spinning cube in react-three-fiber but the page slows down over time and the cube speed changes depending on the monitor. Here's my component: it uses useState for rotation inside useFrame and creates a new THREE.Color in the JSX props every render. Fix it and explain what was wrong.
evals.expected_output: Rewritten component using useRef mutation in useFrame with delta-scaled rotation, no setState per frame, memoized/static color prop, explanation of frame-rate independence and R3F golden rules.

evals.prompt: My Three.js scene renders the glTF model way too dark and the metal parts are completely black, plus textures I load myself with TextureLoader look washed out compared to Blender. Using MeshStandardMaterial and a DirectionalLight. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis: missing scene.environment (IBL) so metals reflect nothing, missing tone mapping (ACESFilmic/AgX/Neutral), and TextureLoader color maps need texture.colorSpace = SRGBColorSpace. Concrete corrected code.

evals.prompt: Render 20,000 animated grass blades swaying in the wind at 60fps in Three.js. It currently uses 20,000 separate Mesh objects and runs at 4fps.
evals.expected_output: InstancedMesh (one draw call) with per-instance matrices, wind via vertex shader (onBeforeCompile or TSL positionNode) driven by a time uniform and per-instance variation, not per-frame CPU matrix updates; mentions frustum culling caveat.
